_extracted.professional_summary: Experienced professional with commitment and academic grounding in international development; and expertise in Women, Peace, and Security (WPS) at multiple levels - from grassroots implementation to research and publishing. Experience in leading high-impact projects that advance sustainable development and empower marginalised communities, in collaboration with international organisations. focusing on critical areas such as gender equality and expanding economic and educational opportunities for minorities.

employment.description_of_duties: Directed the organisation's efforts to combat gender-based violence in the Afar region of Ethiopia through programmes that promote girls' enrolment in education and post-secondary pathways, as well as initiatives advancing women's financial inclusion by establishing a women's cooperatives. In the challenging context of the civil war of Ethiopia, I pivoted towards addressing the gendered impacts of conflict and led war emergency response efforts including the delivery of essential medical equipment to those heavily affected by war.

Founded Lem Chaka, a company that promotes circular economy through the recycling of food waste sourced from hotels in Addis Ababa to produce high-quality organic fertilizers. Led a team of 12 people to establish the company from the ground up. Ensured that each initiative went beyond environmental responsibility; and tackled extreme poverty and the inclusion of the most vulnerable groups in every project. Recipient of a $246,000 grant from USAID for the implementation of CapEx equipments.

employment.employer: Office of the President of Ethiopia
employment.description_of_duties: Collaborated with and reported to H.E. Sahle-Work Zewde, during her tenure as President of Ethiopia, to provide strategic guidance and develop key policy recommendation on gender equality during high-level international conferences. Conferences attended: Global Women Leaders High-Level Dialogue (Madrid, January 2024), High-Level Dialogue on Women in Conflicts in the Great Lake Region (Luanda, October 2024), High-Level Forum on the Future of Africa-Europe Relations (Brussels, November 2024).

employment.employer: African Women Peace and Security Institute
employment.description_of_duties: Gender consultant at the African Women Peace and Security Institute (AWPSI). I fulfilled various roles including research, coordination, and logistical support to build the AWPSI. Led research totalling over 140 pages involving a comparative analysis on the implementation of peace agreements, some spearheaded by women, in conflict-affected nations. Analysed and drew insights from the gendered impacts of conflicts in Afghanistan, Colombia, Liberia, and the DRC resulting in a roadmap proposal for post-conflict Ethiopia.

Consultant at the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa in the Gender, Poverty, and Social Policy (GPSP) Division, focusing on extreme poverty and gender equality. Supported the Chief of the GPSP division in the drafting and preparation of meetings, external presentations and technical proposals.

Consultant at the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights in the Eastern Regional Office (OHCHR-EARO). Co-headed missions related to Djibouti. Fostered collaborations with Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) in Djibouti, engaged in exchanges to empower them, conducted trainings and collectively addressed the challenges they encountered. Contributed to the communications team responsible for weekly reporting to headquarters in Geneva.

_extracted.professional_summary: As a Digital Financial Services (DFS) IT Manager with over 11 years of progressive experience in IT infrastructure, systems administration, and digital financial services, I bring a multifaceted skill set combining technical proficiency, industry knowledge, and managerial expertise. Currently leading digital transformation initiatives at Ethiopost, I have successfully managed complex IT operations, supervised teams, and implemented solutions that enhance organizational productivity and security. My background spans system engineering, senior systems administration, and solutions architecture, with deep expertise in financial technology, cybersecurity, and IT infrastructure design. I am committed to driving secure, efficient, and customer-centric digital banking solutions while ensuring regulatory compliance and managing emerging fintech innovations.

employment.description_of_duties: Develop and implement digital financial products and services, ensuring alignment with business objectives and customer needs. Stay abreast of fintech trends and innovations, integrating new technologies to enhance digital financial services offerings. Ensure regulatory compliance and adherence to financial industry standards in all digital financial services operations. Lead strategic planning efforts for digital financial services, including market analysis, product design, and rollout strategies. Manage partnerships with fintech companies, mobile money operators, financial institutions, and stakeholders to expand digital service offerings. Oversee risk management processes to identify and mitigate cybersecurity threats, fraud risks, and data security issues. Provide leadership to a team of professionals, offering guidance, mentorship, and support for achieving organizational goals. Execute project management methodologies to ensure the successful implementation of digital financial service projects.

Develop solutions for an organization by designing and implementing IT solutions for improving organization's operations. Evaluate business needs and develop solutions to solve issues in the company. Provide recommendations to the company and stakeholders on what type of solutions would maximize their productivity or reduce issues in their operations. Collect relevant documentation to develop technical roadmaps and propose solutions. Oversee the systems administration department within an organization. Supervise Operations team, monitor system performance and configure new software and hardware. Manage systems administrators who oversee day-to-day problems in this managerial role requiring leadership skills and demonstrated experience in systems administration. Perform cost-benefit and return-on-investment analyses for proposed systems to aid management in making implementation decisions. Interact and negotiate with vendors, outsourcers, and contractors to secure system-related products and services. Develop and implement backup and recovery strategies to protect data and ensure business continuity.

Oversee the development, installation, configuration, and administration of computing systems. Troubleshoot technical issues and develop effective solutions. Keep abreast of the latest advancements in technology, protocols, and best practices. Apply proven communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills to help identify, communicate, and resolve issues to maximize the benefit of IT systems in the organization. Ensure the stability, integrity, and efficient operation of in-house information systems that support core organizational functions. Develop and implement backup and recovery strategies to protect data and ensure business continuity.

employment.description_of_duties: As the Project Team Leader for the Ethiopia Connect Project at AMAS, I play a pivotal role in driving strategic initiatives to enhance financial inclusion, particularly access to finance for smallholder farmers in Ethiopia. My approach involved identifying funding sources that would support the growth and scaling of seedling operations. My leadership and communication skills were key in building strong partnerships and ensuring the success of these initiatives.

employment.employer: Bilateral Program between Netherlands Government, Embassy of Kingdom of Netherlands (EKN) and Ethiopian Government, Ministry of Finance
employment.description_of_duties: BENEFIT used to test and validate technologies, practices, and approaches as well as build the capacity of relevant stakeholders in agriculture and trade in partnership with local universities, agriculture research institutes, agriculture offices and public and private enterprises. The program is implemented in Ethiopia with support of WAGENINGEN University and Research (WUR) in four regions (Amhara, Oromia, Tigray and SNNPR). There were five programs under BENEFIT umbrella and my responsibility in BENEFIT is to ensure gender and Nutrition Sensitive Agriculture is embedded during planning, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of each program, such as Integrated Seed Sector Development (ISSD), Capacity Building and Scaling Up of Evidence Based Approach (CASCAPE), Sesame Business Network Development project (SBN), Ethiopian Netherlands Trade for Agricultural Growth (ENTAG) and Realizing Sustainable Agricultural Livelihood Security (REALISE). Gender and Nutrition Mainstreaming and Specific strategies Responsible for developing gender and nutrition mainstreaming policy, strategies, standards, norms and guidance/technical for the BENEFIT Partnership. Ensure gender and nutrition is embedded in the different programs of the BENEFIT Partnership. And supports gender analysis and tailor-made support for each project. Support the nutrition-agriculture linkages and contribute to the development of the annual work plan and technical decisions as well as for program management, implementation, and monitoring, in compliance with the defined individual BENEFIT programs strategies and approaches. B. Sensitization and Communication Promote nutrition and gender sensitive learning and innovations among and outside the BENEFIT Partnership units. This includes promoting networking and partnership with government and partners to promote gender and nutrition in their programs and interventions via different platforms. Provide gender and nutrition trainings and coaching to BENEFIT staff and partners involved. I'm responsible for regular visit to regional staff to engage on gender and nutrition thematic areas. In collaboration with the M & E and Communication, develop appropriate gender and nutrition measurement indicators and track progress and success stories based on the established indicators. Develop tailored messages sharing the results of BENEFIT on gender and nutrition with various audiences. Provide regular advice to the BENEFIT Management team on gender and nutrition issues and get their collaboration to strengthen the two themes in their intervention. C. Monitoring and Evaluation and Reporting Follow up of the implementation of the proposed activities and documentation of the best practices in mainstreaming gender in programs. This includes conducting regular gender and nutrition impact audit together with the M & E expert and in consultation with the BENEFIT management Team. Together with BENEFIT manager and projects' staff on gender and nutrition, feed gender and nutrition results into a collaboration monitoring framework. Responsible for internal and external evaluation of all projects and programs under the BENEFIT Partnership. Accountable for tracking progresses and publicizing the results achieved and communicating to financer and other relevant stakeholders. I should also consolidate the monitoring and analysis of the collaboration. Produce quarterly progress reports and annual reports on gender sensitive nutrition.

Collected and promoted good practices and lessons learned, contributing to a collection of applicable good practices for gender equality programming.

Recruited, supervised, coached EHPEA gender and health team, Coordinate and lead the development of annual plan and budget, provided trainings for farm management on gender and gender mainstreaming, facilitated workshops, and edutainment programs, Supported farms to develop a gender committee and gender policy at farm level, harassment policy & procedure, (support includes development and provision of various tools and close follow up and supervision), Followed up the benchmarking of social requirements against international standards and EHPEA Code of Practice, Developed and maintained sustainable partnerships and alliances with Civil Society organizations and Social Movements to advance EHPEA's work on gender and health, Capitalized success stories promoting gender work to internal and external audiences.

Planned and organized incoming and outgoing visits, missions, duty travel, seminars, and exhibitions, Set agenda and arranged meetings for the counsellor, various appointments for the counsellor to achieve his mission, Handled timely response of correspondences and made sure that timely responded, Coordinated and facilitated logistics for delegates and missionaries, Handled financial management, administration, archiving, logistics in the office, Handled phone calls and receiving visitors

I used to be a member of change management Directorate in FEACC. The Directorate was established following the business process re-engineering conducted in the commission in 2009. I was promoted to as a change agent, and my responsibilities were: 1. Communicate and promote the benefits of organizational change at all levels in line with the new vision of the organization. 2. Encourage participatory ways of working with relevant stakeholders such that there is a sustained level of engagement and buy-in of the new organizational vision statement. 3. Set clear and measurable objectives of organizational change actions with milestones and check their progress on a continuous basis through self-assessment; contribute to monitoring the implementation of the change process. 4. Develop work plans to facilitate the implementation of organizational change in the relevant change areas. 5. Facilitate the development of practical proposals for organizational change in line with the vision statement and strategic framework to address the organizational issues and advise / propose mechanisms for implementation to achieve full ownership. 6. Communicate clearly at all levels during each step of the organizational change process. 7. Assist in providing both advice and concrete suggestions to the strategic management body or board of directors responsible for supporting the organizational change initiative. 8. I used to be a member of the Commission's management committee and used to take minute of the management committee meeting. Actively engaged and ensured gender mainstreaming in strategic and annual plan preparation and implementation of the commission. Facilitated and organized in-house and external gender related trainings to the Commission staff and management members so as to enhance gender sensitivity in the commissions. Celebrated gender related events such as 16 days activism against GBV and March 8 to created awareness and advocated and lobbied the management to put in place gender sensitive organizational policies. Promoted local and international experiences regarding integration of gender in anti-corruption activities for colleagues and top management members. Undertook joint regular monitoring and evaluation to identify gender gaps. Participated on the regular monitoring to assess the progress and proper implementation of gender and gender sensitive policies, programs and systems. Organized mentoring and backstopping sessions to improve the gap identified during monitoring and evaluation. Participated in research activities conducted in the area of gender and anti-corruption, such as gender audit. Developed sex disaggregated data gathering system together with Data administrators. Published plans, activities and achievements regarding gender in the Commission's newsletter. Used to organize training to empower women staff in the Commission, such as women in leadership, women assertiveness and decision making, etc.

_extracted.professional_summary: Result oriented, ITIL and PMP certified Information Technology executive with more than 21 years of proven track record of delivering IT business solutions and operations across commercial and government institutions. Currently working as Data/IT Advisor within the Ethiopia Ministry of Revenues Tax Transformation Program, responsible for delivering tax transformation projects designed to improve the ease of doing business (eodb) ranking of Ethiopia, specifically improvements in paying tax. Previously served as Chief IT Officer (CIO) and E-Trading Project Manager within Ethiopia Commodity Exchange, where successfully managed the software requirement analysis, design, development and implementation of the entire commodity trading platform software application (Electronic Trading Software) from ground-up for Africa's fastest growing Commodity Exchange in Ethiopia. Possesses more than 8 years of experience as a software developer, having worked in Ethiopia Commodity Exchange and USAID/AED Project.

Provide technical support on design and maintenance of existing business application software.

Ensure the integrity, availability, and confidentiality of ECX business and corporate applications. Analyze ECX's current technology infrastructure and align it with the business' strategic plans. Develop ECX's IT strategy, IT policies and establish the IT department's operative goals and objectives. Ensure that ECX maintains a robust workforce of well-qualified IT professionals. Ensure that new application development, maintenance, and enhancement activities are managed and delivered in an efficient and cost-effective manner. Checks and administers contractual agreements until contract closeout. Follows the activities of project management team, provide insights and motivation to members of the team. Assesses and oversees the general progress of project against project plan, strategic plans and vision & mission of ECX. Examines various reports prepared from Work Performance Information to see activities are generally cohesive to objectives and scope. Designed, developed and implemented an electronic commodity exchange trading platform software and databases including Electronic trading, warehouse receipt system, central depository, clearing house, & secure electronic bank gateway using Microsoft ASP.NET, C#, SQL Server. Developed and maintained a sound and integrated IT application software development architecture. As an IT project Manager, successfully managed many IT projects including online Trading Platform development Project with a total project budget of 3.1 million USD. Built a data center and secure IT infrastructure to fully network local & remote delivery centers without a single incident of trade session cancellation. Built a zero default system processing over $10Billion USD in trade volume to-date without a single error and settlement of all transactions on a trade day plus one basis. Built a robust market data feed enabling ECX market data system to disseminate market data in real-time using SMS, IVR, Pricetickers, Market Information Kiosks(MIKs) and ECX website, making market information instantly available to rural farmers and other market actors. Developed a blueprint aligned with ECX strategy and began implementing robust and scalable data center infrastructure using best of breed technologies for servers, storage solutions, network security and disaster recovery mechanisms to support aggressive business expansion. Represented ECX in local and international media including presenting papers at international conferences.

employment.position: Deputy CIO, Data Warehouse and IT Infrastructure
employment.description_of_duties: Build a new data center and securing IT infrastructure to fully network ECX's local & remote delivery centers without a single incident of trade session cancellation. Designing and implementing an in-house data warehousing software application. Manage and administer the daily data warehousing activities. Design, develop and implement various reports using Business intelligence tools. Manage and build a team of network engineers responsible for the existing network infrastructure both LAN and WAN. Lead the team that is responsible for providing IT security. Manage and lead the development of the newly designed data warehouse system. Lead the implementation of the data center upgrade project. Providing employee development, coaching, mentoring and team building. Monitor reliability and availability of all data and ensure work according to benchmark standards. Analyze and recommend changes to increase productivity level of data warehouse in working environment. Administer database consolidation and identify efficient initiatives for same. Maintain the data warehouse and reports to ensure they are published on time and that the information published is accurate.

Develop architectural and low level design for the development of an in-house designed commodity exchange software used for trading, clearing and settlement of daily business operations. Providing technical guidance to the software development and ensuring that sound software engineering design process and quality practices are followed. Analyzing and reviewing the design of the software, including technical reviews and analysis of source code. Responsible for the delivery of software applications within budget, schedule, quality and corporate guidelines. Analyzing and writing application software requirements, functional / design specifications and test plans. Analyzing appropriate technologies, vendor products, competitor's offerings, options for critical design elements and features and determining optimum selection. Build Software development team from the ground up. Recruited top-notch developers in a very competitive market. Building a culture promoting technical excellence. Managing and building a team of architects, developers responsible for developing and maintaining integrated commodity exchange software developed with Microsoft .NET platform, including C#, ADO.NET, ASP.NET and Microsoft SQL Server. Providing employee development, coaching, mentoring and team building.

Designed, developed and implemented ECX-clearing and settlement application software which was used by the exchange as a tool for clearing and settlement of daily trade transactions. Before upgrading this software, a total of more than 1 billion dollars traded with 0 defaults. This software had the functionality to link with all the clearing banks so that balance, pay-in and payout instruction will be requested from the settlement banks automatically in a secure way. The clearing module of this software calculates the total net obligation per each member and client that traded for the specified trade date. It also calculates fees and charges that the exchange deducts from each trade. The software has another section which is responsible for calculating the net obligation and settlement instruction for inventory financed commodities. Designed, developed and implemented ECX-trading application software which the trading department uses as a tool for processing trade data. Submitted trades will be verified with more than 25 trade status checks. It has a module which is optimized for fast and automatic verification of each trade which based on this verification a trade becomes accepted or rejected. The software is capable of identifying trades that does not meet the exchange's requirements. Designed and developed ECX-warehouse application. The software was used in ECX warehouses in order to sample, grade, load and create Good Receiving Notice (GRN). It has also another section for tracking Good Issue Notice (GIN) information. Maintained and enhanced the above developed software application as required by the exchange.

Designed and developed Decision Support system that shows the previous year's trend analysis on key educational indicators including Net Intake Rate, Gross Enrollment Rate, teachers demand, and Classroom demand. Data is extracted and analyzed using the ETL (Extract Transform and Load) system from the data warehouse. Designed, developed and implemented Enrollment Projection Model software at national level which is used in all regional education bureaus of Ethiopia. This software plays an important role in determining the future enrollment demand in the country. It will also project the associative inputs like teacher demand, classroom demand textbook demand etc. Currently, it is used as a tool in plan preparation for achieving Universal Primary Education in Ethiopia. Designed and developed Project Monitoring Software for Ministry of Education. The system was developed to monitor all projects that operate under the Ministry of Education. Both Physical Performance and financial performance of each project was monitored using this software. Designed, developed and implemented customized educational software which was used as a tool in Woreda capacity building program held in all woreda of the country. Designed, developed and implemented Material Management Information System software. MMIS is used in all regional education bureaus to manage and control their stores. It is interactive and has every feature like issue, receive, discard etc.

Designed, developed school performance management software for Tigray Regional Education Bureau. This software considers all the primary schools in the region and categorizes the schools based on their performance on key educational indicators like dropout rate and repetition rate. Designed and developed Minimum Quality Standard for Access and distribution of schools software for the Regional Education Bureau. This software has been used in determining the future number of schools to be constructed taking into account School size, a radius that a student travels to the nearest school and school-aged population in the region. There are others different educational software developed during the period Jan 01-Dec 03. Such as Decision Support system that shows the previous trend analysis on key educational indicators.

_extracted.professional_summary: Petros Mulugeta Yigzaw is a development and investment professional with 6 years of experience in project management, business development, and investment facilitation across Ethiopia and East Africa. Currently serving as Deputy Head of Office and Project Manager for Economic and Scientific Cooperation at the Bavarian Office for Africa/GIZ InS, he represents Bavaria's development, economic, scientific, and cultural cooperation across five African focus countries. He has extensive expertise in industrial park development, investment promotion, business planning, feasibility studies, and market research. With a strong background in conducting qualitative and quantitative research, managing complex stakeholder engagements, and designing impactful development projects, Petros combines technical competencies in project management tools, data analysis, and content development with deep knowledge of Ethiopia's investment ecosystem and broader African development priorities.

employment.employer: Bavarian Office for Africa / GIZ InS
employment.position: Deputy Head of Office / Project Manager for Economic and Scientific Cooperation
employment.description_of_duties:
The office represents the free state of Bavaria across four cooperation areas which are development, economic, scientific, and cultural within the five focus African countries (Ethiopia, Senegal, Kenya, Tunisia, and South Africa). My responsibilities include:
• Conceive, initiate, and develop cooperation projects in areas such as TVET support, forestry, business development, agriculture and manufacturing industry development, health, and more.
• As a representative of the financier, I'm responsible for reviewing project proposals from potential implementers, monitoring and evaluating projects financed by the Bavarian state across the 5 focus countries, assessing their socio-economic impact, and making sure they align with project goals and efficient resource utilization.
• Consulting Bavarian SMEs interested in expanding to Africa, providing insight into regional markets.
• Creating and sending frequent reports on the development of strategic sectors such as green hydrogen and other renewables, automotive (e-mobility in particular), and finance to the Bavarian Ministry of Economic Affairs.
• Creating links between Ethiopian/ African and Bavarian Institutions (such as universities, research institutions, government offices, and CSOs) and facilitating their collaboration.
• Representing and promoting Ethiopia and Africa in economic forums and events in Bavaria such as the Bavaria-Africa forum, IFAT, and others.
• Monitoring, researching, and reporting on political and socio-economic developments across the focus countries and the African Union.
• Organization of cultural events and delegation trips to Africa by the Bavarian State Government or the Bavarian Parliament

As part of the Sustainable Industrial Clusters (S.I.C.) program, I was mainly tasked with developing a new manual for Industrial Parks One Stop Shop (OSS) service centers and conducting market research on available operational OSS platforms. Hence, I had to engage with a lot of public and private stakeholders (EIC, ECC, MoR, CBE, CIS, Ethio-Telecom, EEU, ETIDI, IPDC, DHL and Pan Afrique Ethiopia).

• Developing comprehensive project proposals as lead researcher on behalf of TCC. Some of our work include, proposals for a regional poultry sector development project by the Amhara regional government to develop business models and an investment case for selected intervention areas in the poultry sector.
• Drafting inception reports, designing surveys, overseeing data collection, conducting interviews and focus group discussions, coding and analysis of qualitative data. I have been a part of research spanning various subjects such as impact of covid on the Ethiopian MICE industry, e-governance and doing business, and constraints blocking domestic investors from entering IPs.
• Conduct feasibility studies and create investment decks.

• Consulting investors on the possible investment avenues in Ethiopia (pros and cons), and the procedures involved in licensing and registration and different services provided by relevant institutions.
• Developing business plans for enterprises in pre-implementation and expansion phases. I have worked on business plans for companies in the textile, construction material manufacturing, industrial parks development, leather and real-estate sectors.
• I was the content manager for IPortal, an online platform designed to provide detailed information on the investment process in Ethiopia (especially for FDI). I have drafted a content strategy and taken part in writing and editing over 30 articles for the website. As IPortal is a platform designed to provide information on the investment process in Ethiopia, the articles are focused on investor FAQs and the steps investors (and especially FDI) will have to go through to become operational in Ethiopia (beginning from arriving in Ethiopia). A Sample of articles I have written: https://investin.et/industrial-park-developer/, https://investin.et/the-investment-climate-in-ethiopia/
• As the content manager I have hired and manage a team of writers and legal analysts. I have designed a training program (including introduction to investment in Ethiopia, copywriting and search engine optimization), actively monitor their progress, and manage their tasks using digital tools such as click up and slack.

• Develop a baseline assessment tools for the Tristate IGA project: the project is designed to provide training and funding to women from low-income families.
• Conduct a market opportunity assessment in areas the women live in and support them in setting up a business of their choice.
• Assist in the financial literacy and soft-skills training provided for the women.
• Follow up and conduct a bimonthly assessment to report on their progress and challenges they are facing.

• Reviewing the feasibility studies of various potential industrial park entrants guided them through developing a feasibility study when needed. When found to have fulfilled all entry requirements, presenting the projects to the deputy commissioner of the Industrial parks division for approval
• Facilitating the incorporation of entrants into Industrial Parks. This involves investor hand holding throughout the pre-implementation, implementation and operational stages. Some common tasks include providing information such as investment and industrial park laws, incentives available to them and procedural requirements at different government; and accompanying them to different institutions (such as NBE, IPDC, and EFDA) representing EIC. I have worked with many of the new companies entering Kilinto Industrial Park, Adama Industrial Park and the Integrated Agro-Industrial Parks.
• Served as a park lead for Hawassa and Vogue Industrial Parks. Furthermore, I was appointed key account manager for over 36 companies. Through this role I have represented and supported Industrial Parks tenants in negotiating with different government institutions to solve their issues. INVEA, Customs Commission, Ethiopian Food and Drug Authority and Industrial Parks Development Corporation are some of the institutions I regularly dealt with.
• Prepared and provided presentations on different industrial parks to foreign investors and taken them on tour of various IPs.
• Consulting duties in the drafting of the Industrial Parks land allocation directive.
• Initiated and co-led the Industrial Parks Division Profiling project. This project was aimed at mapping the history of the IP Division within EIC, its key achievements and shortcomings, challenges, mandates of the directorates within the division (as there was some clashing of mandates at the time), collaborations with other divisions and institutions and recommendations to fill the gaps.

This invaluable experience not only strengthened my leadership skills but also fostered my problem-solving abilities and effective communication techniques and above all it fueled drive in me for both therapeutic and diagnostic research in our setting that will one day cure their disease.

I regularly provided updates and presentations to our clinic staffs regarding the services we were delivering.
This service has meaningful and significant impact on the patients quality of life. We saw a total of 78 patients in the first 6 months only. Most patients were advanced cancer patients.

Developing improvement strategies: Collaborating with stakeholders to create innovative and effective solutions that address identified challenges and enhance patient outcomes.
By providing expert guidance and support, I contribute to the ongoing advancement of healthcare quality and patient safety.

This included delivering lectures, conducting rounds and bedside teachings, moderating seminars, preparing and conducting written and practical exams.
I served as a mentor for medical students, acting as a preceptor for at least two students in each group.
I collaborated closely with the program director to the overall growth of the program.
As a General practitioner, I have gained a wealth of knowledge and experience. I have taken on various responsibilities and worked in different departments, including Medical wards, ICU, Emergency, palliative care and ART(anti retroviral treatment) departments.
I have had ample opportunity to work in both inpatient and outpatient settings, performing procedures, leading rounds, and managing teams.
Additionally, I have presented cases in morning sessions, mortality and morbidity sessions, and provided updates on the management of specific cases such as COVID-19, Diabetes, several Oncologic cases like gastric cancer and Asthma management.

employment.position: Gender Youth and Social Inclusion and Psychosocial support Lead
employment.description_of_duties: Led a team on MESMER program which is a program funded by MasterCard Foundation and is implemented by First Consult PLC. The MESMER program works on putting in place an access to finance, business development, and psychosocial services to help MSMEs recover from shocks, build resilience and grow to create dignified and fulfilling work for the youth. From the design of the proposal phase involved in the implementation of the 4 components mainly leading the Psychosocial and the Business development of MESMER program. Interventions within MESMER are focused putting a system for MSMEs recover from shocks and bridge the financial gap for MSMEs by expanding the availability of affordable credit and enhancing the creditworthiness of MSMEs through BDS, Psychosocial support (PSS), and TA to financial institutions. Developed a gender strategy for MESMER program in order to actualize the reach of young women target (80%) for the coming 5 years. Mainstreaming gender and inclusion aspect of the program within the four components and project activities address gender equality, women's empowerment and positive youth development. Provide technical and managerial oversight to all the team and regional staffs on the access to finance aspect of grant and credit, PSS and BDS along with gender and youth specialists based in all regions the program covers. Ensure that gender and youth is integrated and mainstreamed across all value chains which are selected for the project. Conduct studies, assessment, and document to identify challenges and opportunities in the program and prepare a strategy and recommendation to address the challenges. Special focus on the conflict affected regions to avail skills and promoting resilience in MSMEs to recover from and adapt to the effects of conflict through a robust PSS delivery and make MSMEs enhance their life skills to be productive in running their businesses. Design and implement PSS intervention like group and individual counselling for MSMEs to bounce back from the collapse of markets, the breaking of supply chains and the depletion of workforce capacity. As the program targets 80% women a special women economic empowerment capacity building are being given which also encompasses reproductive health and gender-based violence trainings.

BRIDGES is a Mastercard Foundation funded program which focus on job creation and private sector development program working on youth employability skills mainly on Special Economic Zone (SEZ) areas. Being part of the management team and involved from its proposal design phase, was part of the investment committee to approve concept notes on new and innovative intervention and budget for the intervention presented from all BRIDGES team. By implementing the IP recruitment database system, the BRIDGES program was able to present a systemic and informative tool for policy makers to have access to predictable and reliable labour market information and products. The private sector was also able to rely on the system which facilitated and eased the recruitment system in the industrial parks. Design and implement a targeted retention mechanism within factories through the development of soft skill training programs and curriculum aimed at easing the transition of workers into the Industrial workforce. As 95% of the beneficiaries are women, the soft skill training curriculum was designed from gender sensitive which is focused on gender equality and empowerment for women. Develop a policy briefing with recommendation on the expulsion of Ethiopia from AGOA and the economic contribution of foreign direct investment and Industrial Parks to Ethiopia. Held a workshop with Ethiopian Investment Commission management and other stakeholders and disused the way forward. Working closely with Industrial Park Development Corporation (IPDC) was able to develop an affordable housing document that identify the issues that hinder the implementation of past studies on affordable housing and attempts at developing housing for industrial park workers in Ethiopia where it also proposes a workable solution. The document was presented to all stakeholders in order to inform the policy that IPDC was drafting on affordable housings for IP workers. Closely work Stakeholder, like the private sector both FDI and local, government and other donor programs, design and implement the efficiency of the worker recruitment platform for industrial Parks across Ethiopia. By addressing quantitative and information mismatch issues in the recruitment process of the Industrial Park labour database aids efficient employment matching and tracks trends in: IP-specific key labour metrics; labour demand and supply; turnover; and demographic information. Design and implement a worker force development and placement for People with disability in different sectors partnering with Ethiopian centre for Disability and Development (ECDD). The intervention focuses on capacitating young women and men with disability to access decent jobs in the labour market by availing information and capacity building for the youth by making them employable. Deliver numerous capacity building for First Consult staff by conducting training on gender mainstreaming and responsive gender budgeting for all interventions implemented by the programs.

_extracted.professional_summary: Mussie Tsegaye is a development consultant with multidisciplinary expertise in Ease of Doing Business, investment facilitation, private sector development, digitalization, project management, and digital entrepreneurship. With extensive experience in the design, implementation, and monitoring of development cooperation programmes specific to economic development, industrialization, and digitalization in Ethiopia, he has worked with numerous government institutions at both federal and local levels and multiple international donors including the EU, World Bank, GIZ, FCDO, and BMGF. He possesses a comprehensive understanding of the political, economic, and organizational dynamics of development projects and their implementation in the region. With a wide network within the public and private sector ecosystem in Ethiopia, he brings hands-on experience in research and implementation of projects across multiple sectors including EODB, agriculture, agro-processing, textile and apparel manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, and ICT. He holds a Master of Arts in Development Studies with a Business Specialization from Mekelle University and complementary technical qualifications in electrical and computer engineering and software engineering.

employment.position: STC - Ease of Doing Business Specialist
employment.description_of_duties:
I support the National Ease of Doing Business Technical and Steering Committees in crafting the second Ease of Doing Business Agenda. Specifically:
- I provided technical support to the Technical Committee in assessing the status of previous Ease of Doing Reforms in terms of current status, impact and way forward
- I supported the Technical Committee convert issues raised through different Public Private Dialogue Platforms into actionable reform items that will be part of the second Ease of Doing Business Reform Plan of the Government of Ethiopia
- I supported the coordination of EoDB implementing institutions on a continuous basis in monitoring and reporting to the technical and steering committees
- I supported technical committee in compiling and presenting weekly reform updates and following up on the decisions of the steering committee

employment.employer: EU Funded GIZ InS Project, Business regulatory reforms and investment climate
employment.description_of_duties:
The EU funded BEIC project aims to improve the Ease of Doing Business and Investment Climate of Ethiopia. My role as an EODB Expert included the assessment, improvement and development of different EODB initiatives of the government including:

− Supporting the Assessment, proposal and enhancement/development of different EODB initiatives of the government including digital service provision E-Portals
− Support the 'Starting a Business' EODB Indicator in terms of diagnosis and support on Online Trade Registration and Licensing System of the Ministry of Trade and Regional Integration, propose upgrades to the system in order to improve the ranking in terms of reducing the number of days to start a business. Specifically, our team proposed and implemented an integration with different stakeholders in the business registration process including MoTRI, DARA, National ID, CCIB Issuing bodies and MoR.
− Support the 'Paying Taxes' EODB Indicator in terms of diagnosis and support on e-Filing and e-Payment solutions for tax administration system at the Ministry of Revenues. Specifically, my team supported the MoR in improving the indicator through the upgrade of the E-Filing system significantly reducing the file upload speed and digitalizing the Clearance and Refund systems among other support areas.
− Support the 'Access to Credit' EODB Indicator on the enhancement/improvement of Credit Registry Bureau and Movable Collateral Asset Registry systems at the Central Bank of Ethiopia. Specifically, our team diagnosed the existing Credit Bureau and Movable Collateral Asset registry systems and supported the NBE in improving its credit scoring activities.
− Support on Training Needs Analysis, capacity building and strategy development for improvement of service provision pertaining to EoDB indicators and cross cutting digital e-services activities at different ministries including the MINT, MOR, NBE and MoTRI
− Provide capacity building on different EODB services provision and digital capacity building activities for all ministries including technical, project management, security, cybersecurity trainings (including training of trainers) and study tours to two destinations

employment.employer: Envest Technologies PLC (formerly Envest ICT Consultancy)

We are a member of Alibaba's eFounders Fellowship Program for Africa (2020/21) and China Merchant Group's C-STAR Programme (2023). As of June, 2024, we are a designated Africa branch and secretary general office for Ethiopia and Djibouti of the Shenzhen Cross-Border E-Commerce Association which is the largest Cross-Border E-Commerce association of more than 3000 Chinese E-Commerce Companies and we work with E-Commerce Companies to improve the E-Commerce Ecosystem in Ethiopia and Beyond.

employment.position: Private Sector Development Specialist, Competitive Neutrality Assessment on the Agricultural Sector in Ethiopia (Fertilizer and Seed Sectors)
employment.description_of_duties:
I supported the private sector analysis (including data collection) of research on the fertilizer and hybrid seed value chains in Ethiopia, the study included the following aspects of both State-Owned Enterprises (SOEs) and private actors in the two sectors:

− Background on the sector: market size, trends, structure, main players, sector prospects, challenges and regulatory environment of the seed and fertilizer sectors
− Value chain assessment and analysis of market characteristics (including production, distribution and sales, prices, principal characteristics of downstream product markets, with particular focus on the role of SOEs vis a vis private sector players along the value chain).
− Assessment of government interventions (regulations/policies) that may restrict competition along the seed value chain
− Policy recommendation to improve the competitiveness of the two markets based on the inefficiencies identified through the assessments

employment.position: Industry Park Services Advisor and Digitalization Expert (Team Leader)

Developed an Industrial Park Consolidated One Stop Shop Manual including:
- Mapping the detailed process flows including steps and required documents for more than 160 services being provided by more than 15 service providers for investors in Industrial Parks
- Organizing the process maps into a consolidated OSS Manual document which is being used as a reference for any investor who plans to or has already invested in Ethiopia.

Undertook a Market Research, Business Process Analysis and system requirement specification preparation for OSS Digital System including:
- Analysis of existing systems which are currently providing online services to investors by more than 15 service providers across more than 10 special economic zones including the services provided, specific functions, gaps, integration between systems a as well as proposed improvements for a seamless service provision to investors.
- Detail assessment and gap analysis of new and existing services as well as business processes that should be re-engineered in order to be integrated into or interfaced with an envisaged OSS digital system

employment.position: Lead Consultant – Study on the challenges and opportunities for domestic firms in Industrial Parks (Textile, Agro-processing and Pharmaceutical sectors)
employment.description_of_duties:
− Lead a study to identify challenges and opportunities in Special Economic Zones for domestic investors in Ethiopia.
− Undertake a survey and qualitative assessment of more than 25 firms in the Textile and Apparel, Agro-processing and pharmaceutical sectors within and out of Industrial parks to identify opportunities and the most pressing challenges in the process of investing in Industrial Parks in Ethiopia
− Undertake a survey and analysis of multiple public institutions including the Investment Commission, Ministry of Industry, Industrial Parks Development Corporation and others to flesh out the critical bottlenecks that investors face and validate potential recommendations to boost domestic investment in Industrial Parks
− Propose policy, regulatory and operational recommendations based on the findings and pitch to the relevant government bodies, primarily the Ministry of Industry

− Liaise with the EIC and Ministry of Foreign Affairs to float Bole Lemi II and Kilinto as a proposal ready for leasing to overseas Ethiopian Embassies and to foreign missions in Addis.
− Provide support to an international WB outreach Textile and Apparel and Pharmaceuticals teams in terms of gathering and compiling critical information required to pitch and attract investors from different government institutions
− Due diligence on potential international investors and follow up on investor queries in order to create a pipeline and facilitate information exchange with the relevant authorities with the ultimate aim of supporting investors to set up in Kilinto and Bole Lemi II Industry Parks

I led the execution of the project including operations, finance and procurement, project monitoring and reporting. The centre was established to provide different trainings to industry park tenants and factory personnel on quality assurance and supervision with the ultimate aim of improving production and productivity of firms within the park.
− Engaged in the operationalization of the Jobs Compact program which is a World Bank, DFID, and EU funded program aiming to hire 30,000 refugees and 70,000 Ethiopians within and beyond Industrial parks. I was involved in the establishment of the program monitoring unit (including recruitment and procurement).
− Engaged in the implementation of a Bill and Melinda Gates foundation funded project with aims of strengthening investor engagement and regulatory powers of the commission. I was responsible for implementation, periodic fund utilization and activity report pertaining to the project.

I led and was also involved in ToR preparation and tender dossier preparation, evaluation and procurement of different activities under the project.

− Play a role in a restructuring of the commission. I was part of a team that diagnosed the institutional arrangement of the commission and proposed an adjustment to the existing structure of the commission.
− Play a major role in the design and implementation of a DFID (UK Aid) funded Young Professional's Program. I was part of the team that designed the program, recruited 15 young professionals and implemented the program.

− Manage and upgrade the structure and content of the Commission's Website. I worked closely with the ICT and PR directorates to upgrade the website and integrate different functionalities including analytics, client feedback, and keyword optimization.
− Design and prepare a bi-monthly investment newsletter for internal staff consumption.
− Support in the Identification and screening of prospective international textile and apparel manufacturers in line with the Ethiopian Investment strategy.
− Play a supporting role in the initiation and establishment of a new strategic management unit at the commission. I was closely involved in the planning, proposal, staffing and operationalization of the unit. This included the diagnosis of the Planning and Reporting system within the commission.

− Develop and deployed content management systems and websites for the company
− Work in a group that specialized in data mining and analysis using machine learning algorithms, specifically I worked on chat bots responsible for medical diagnosis and keeping company of old age patients.

− Installing and configuring network infrastructure
− Developing and Deploying content management systems and websites
− Providing training and support to end-users
− Presenting sales pitch to prospective clients

employment.description_of_duties: Implemented AI tools for the inspection of exterior damage on cars, reducing the time required for preparing condition reports from 12 minutes to 3 minutes. This work streamlined the inspection process, boosting productivity and enhancing accuracy in damage assessment. Conducted in-depth research on improving 3D reconstruction, pose estimation and novel view synthesis techniques from sparse observations.

Developed a multi-stage machine learning model to replace the traditional vision pipeline that had been in place for over five years, enhancing system reliability and performance. Architected a scenario-based evaluation framework to enhance the explainability and interpretability of in-house machine learning models. This framework improved model transparency and aided in the validation and adjustment of model outputs, making them more understandable and actionable for end-users. Boosted on-edge LiDAR processing capabilities by optimizing firmware and streamlining data throughput, significantly improving 3D data handling efficiency. Created tools for efficient hyperparameter tuning that reduced development cycles and enhanced the machine learning models' responsiveness to dynamic environments.

Developed ultra-low latency pedestrian detection systems for automatic emergency braking (AEB), utilizing event cameras and LiDAR technology to enhance rapid response capabilities. Introduced a method to fuse images with radar data, offering a low-cost alternative to traditional LiDAR systems without sacrificing performance. Generated synthetic datasets through simulations of diverse sensors in the CARLA environment, for use in pretraining large transformer models.

Developed deep learning based detection algorithms for company's novel and cutting-edge solid state LiDARs. Explored and pioneered transformer-based models into existing 3D object detection pipeline, setting a new standard for object recognition accuracy.

Engineered a streamlined real-time 3D object detection system that reduced computational load by more than 80% while maintaining robust performance on the edge. Implemented a critical project on model compression, pruning, quantization, low-rank factorization and distillation, achieving a significant reduction in model size with minimal loss in accuracy, enhancing the deployment capabilities on resource-constrained platforms.

Developed drivers and libraries for interfacing with humanoid robots. Used genetic algorithms for generating realistic facial expressions. Devised systematic methodology for collecting and annotating sequences of emotional speech, facial expressions and body gesture.
